top_stories.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
top_stories.summary: Google disclosed a threat actor used an AI-developed zero-day exploit to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA), marking the first known instance of AI being used in the wild for malicious vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. The attack was attributed to cybercrime threat actors and represents a novel escalation in threats targeting authentication mechanisms.

top_stories.title: Omada Launches Omada Identity Cloud Private for Regulated Enterprises and Government Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched "Omada Identity Cloud Private," a new deployment option that runs the full Omada Identity Cloud platform within the customer's own tenant. The offering is aimed at regulated enterprises and government organizations that require strict data boundary controls and auditability.

top_stories.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
top_stories.summary: Attackers are abusing Windows Phone Link using the CloudZ RAT and a new plugin called Pheno to intercept SMS messages on connected smartphones. The attack enables bypassing of SMS-based two-factor authentication by hijacking the Windows-based bridge between PCs and Android devices.

top_stories.title: Why Changing Passwords Doesn’t End an Active Directory Breach
top_stories.summary: Specops Software has published an explanation of why password resets are insufficient to fully remediate Active Directory breaches. The piece details how cached credentials and Kerberos tickets can allow attackers to maintain authenticated access even after passwords have been changed.

top_stories.title: Google researchers uncover criminal zero-day exploit likely built with AI
top_stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group identified a zero-day exploit, likely developed with AI assistance by a criminal group, that targeted a web-based system administration tool. The exploit enabled attackers to bypass two-factor authentication using valid credentials by exploiting a semantic logic error where a hardcoded trust assumption contradicted the application's authentication enforcement.

news.title: Hackers Trick DigiCert Into Issuing Certificates Used to Sign Malware
news.summary: DigiCert revoked 60 code signing certificates after hackers tricked the certificate authority into issuing them via a malicious support chat attachment. The compromised certificates were used to sign Zhong Stealer malware, requiring DigiCert to revoke the fraudulently obtained signing credentials.

news.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Linux backdoor called PamDOORa that abuses Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) to steal SSH credentials and maintain persistent access. The malware, sold for $1,600 on a Russian cybercrime forum, uses a magic password and specific TCP port combination to bypass normal authentication controls.

news.title: A new frontier: Identity stack evolves for agentic systems
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how the identity stack is evolving to support agentic AI systems. The article explores the shift of identity infrastructure toward functioning as a real-time control plane for autonomous AI agents.

news.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
news.summary: Researchers have disclosed PCPJack, a credential theft framework that targets exposed cloud infrastructure by exploiting five CVEs to spread worm-like across systems. The toolset harvests credentials from cloud, container, developer, productivity, and financial services, then exfiltrates the stolen data through attacker-controlled infrastructure.

news.title: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
news.summary: Microsoft published a blog post on World Passkey Day highlighting its efforts to advance passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords. The post covers Microsoft's push toward passwordless authentication, with a focus on reducing phishing risk and improving sign-in security.

news.title: 5 leading enterprise password managers to consider
news.summary: The article outlines key features enterprise password managers should provide for administrators. It covers available options and capabilities relevant to credential and access management in enterprise environments.

news.title: Semperis Names John Podboy Chief Information Security Officer
news.summary: Semperis has appointed John Podboy as its new Chief Information Security Officer. Podboy will focus on risk, resilience, compliance, and agentic AI-driven cyber defense for the identity-driven cyber resilience company.

news.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
news.summary: Gartner has published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, highlighting that enterprise AI agent adoption is outpacing governance and policy controls. The report addresses the identity security challenge of governing AI agents that operate inside enterprise perimeters with access to systems and data.

news.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an attack using the CloudZ RAT and a previously undocumented plugin called Pheno that exploits Windows Phone Link to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs). The malware is specifically designed to intercept OTPs, directly targeting multi-factor authentication mechanisms used to protect account access.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server is now generally available
news.summary: AWS has announced the general availability of the AWS MCP Server, a managed remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to give AI agents and coding assistants authenticated access to AWS services. The server is part of AWS's Agent Toolkit and provides secure, authenticated connectivity between AI tools and AWS service APIs.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing an attacker with admin privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability poses an enterprise risk by enabling credential theft that could facilitate further unauthorized access.

news.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
news.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits rapidly emerging afterward. One researcher reports zero-day exploitation activity has been occurring for at least a month prior to public disclosure, putting millions of cPanel-managed systems at risk.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022. The attacks compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency through credential theft and social engineering techniques.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying instead on router-level interception to silently steal authentication credentials at scale.
